era_context: Late Bronze Age collapse, mid-period. Egypt under Ramesses III continues to dominate the eastern Mediterranean politically and militarily, though internal economic and administrative strains are mounting. The Hittite Empire has been defunct for ~44 years. The Mycenaean world has collapsed. The Sea Peoples remain active. In China, the Shang dynasty is approaching its final years before the Zhou conquest c. 1046 BCE. This is a period of significant civilizational stress and transformation.

events.title: Ramesses III's eleventh regnal year; temple administration and military organization
events.description: Ramesses III's reign continues with documented administrative and military activity. This year falls within the well-attested period of his rule. Papyri and temple reliefs document ongoing governance, temple administration, and military organization. The Medinet Habu temple complex serves as a record of his achievements and reign.

sources.name: Medinet Habu temple reliefs and inscriptions

events.certainty_note: Ramesses III's regnal year 11 is well-documented. Conventional chronology places this c. 1176 BCE ±2 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1136 BCE is extremely sparse. No specific events are recorded for this year in surviving sources. The entry above represents the broader context of Ramesses III's reign. The absence of specific events for this year is typical for the ancient world.
